channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pentagon Plans $200 Billion Investment Program with Private Bankers to Counter China

item.title: Today’s Dollar to Naira Rates for March 13, 2026
item.description: For people wey dey wonder how dollar dey sell for Naija today, the black market rates don drop. As of Thursday, 12th March 2026, for Lagos parallel market, people dey sell dollar for ₦1450 and dey buy...

item.title: Philippines Extends Visa for Foreigners Affected by Middle East Flight Disruptions
item.description: The Philippine government will provide visa relief to foreigners affected by recent flight cancellations due to the conflict in the Middle East. Following Department of Justice (DOJ) Department Order ...

item.title: Charge of threatening Prime Minister dropped against Nathan Ballesty, lesser offence remains
item.description: Prosecutors have dropped a serious charge against Nathan Ballesty for allegedly threatening to kidnap Prime Minister Anthony Albanese. Ballesty, 27, was arrested in December following a series of offe...

item.title: NPA commits to addressing Tembisa Hospital corruption cases and protecting whistleblowers

item.title: DZRH Reporter Arrested for Publishing Councilor's Arrest Warrant, NUJP Condemns as Retaliatory
item.description: A veteran DZRH radio reporter, Misael “Boy” Gonzales, aged 66, was arrested by Manila Police on March 11, 2026, following a complaint filed by Manila Councilor Rosalino Ibay Jr. The arrest was over an...

item.description: Women entrepreneurs are making big contributions to Tanzania's economy by running businesses that create jobs, feed families, and support local markets. They are active in many sectors, including food...

item.title: Iranian diplomat Mohammad Pournajaf granted asylum in Australia after defection in 2023
item.description: Iran’s second most senior diplomat in Australia, Mohammad Pournajaf, who served as chargé d’affaires at the Iranian embassy in Canberra, defected from the Islamic Republic regime and was granted asylu...

item.title: Parliamentary Proceedings on Budget Session: Lok Sabha Adjourned, Rajya Sabha Discusses Ministries and Committee Reports
item.description: On March 13, 2026, the Budget session of the Indian Parliament witnessed significant developments. The Lok Sabha was adjourned immediately after proceedings began due to Opposition members’ protests a...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Escalates: Military Strikes and Regional Tensions Intensify
item.description: On 13 March 2026, the US-Israel military campaign against Iran continues to escalate, affecting both the region and global markets. A US KC-135 refuelling aircraft crashed in Iraq's friendly airspace,...

item.title: KC-135 U.S. refuelling aircraft crashes in western Iraq, rescue operations ongoing
item.description: A U.S. KC-135 Stratotanker, involved in aerial refuelling operations during the American military campaign against Iran, crashed over western Iraq following an incident with another aircraft, accordin...

item.title: Muturi Alleges Government Pressure on Nairobi Hospital Board
item.description: Nairobi — The Democratic Party leader, Justin Muturi, has publicly accused President William Ruto of trying to take control of Nairobi Hospital through intimidation and pressure on its board. Accordin...

item.title: Alberta Landowners Block Oil Company Over Unpaid Lease Fees
item.description: Some landowners in southwest Edmonton are taking direct action against MAGA Energy, an oil company that has reportedly failed to pay its lease for three years. Mark Dorin and Dale Braun erected a wood...

item.title: Trump Shifts Stance on High Oil Prices Amid Iran War and Strait of Hormuz Disruptions
item.description: President Donald Trump, who previously highlighted low gasoline prices—around $2.30 per gallon in his February 2026 State of the Union address—has changed his rhetoric following the U.S.-initiated war...

item.title: Residents of Phugwani Village Still Face Water Shortages Despite Promises of Borehole
item.description: Residents of Phugwani village near Malamulele in Limpopo are still struggling with water shortages after being promised a borehole before the 2024 elections. According to locals, no municipal water ta...

item.title: Jets struggle defensively in 6-3 defeat to Rangers, playoff hopes diminish
item.description: The Winnipeg Jets suffered another disappointing defeat, losing 6-3 to the New York Rangers on Thursday night. After hoping to rebound from one of their worst performances this season, the Jets quickl...
